process_issues.description: Adding a fork OSC handler requires users to re-run install:remote-script + relaunch Ableton. Mitigated cleanly by installing while Ableton was CLOSED (loads on next launch, no toggle). Documented in manual-smoke.md.

summary: Adherence retro for the leadsheet 'Apply to Ableton' run (PR #36). CLEAN/STRICT run: orchestrator entry → upfront alignment → planning-agent → 2 coding-agents → verification-gate (133 unit, 47 e2e build+packaged, screenshot) → manual smoke PASS first try → failure-postmortem. No repair loops, no W-violations. Notable: BOTH skill improvements from the two prior retros were followed and worked — (1) coding-agents returned validation reports (not premature project-state rewrites), and (2) verification-gate Required Evidence #9 (packaged-runtime parity) was honored (ran test:e2e:build against the real .app for a renderer change). The self-improvement loop is functioning: fixes landed last retro prevented their target failure classes this run.

observed_chain: planning-agent (subagent; investigated project-path mechanism, chose fork handler)
coding-agent x2 (server+fork LS-A/B/C, client LS-D — both returned validation reports per the 2026-06-05 coding-agent fix)
verification-gate (Skill; typecheck/lint/133 unit/build + test:e2e:build packaged #9 + leadsheet screenshot)
manual-smoke PASS (live PNG-write + clips + AbleSet; fork installed while Ableton closed)
